   value of |yocto-ver| above.

## Release notes for |yocto-ver| (|yocto-codename|)

This document lists new features and enhancements for the Yocto Project
|yocto-ver| Release (codename "|yocto-codename|"). For a list of breaking
changes and migration guides, see the /migration-guides/migration-6.0
section.

The |yocto-ver| (|yocto-codename|) release is the new LTS release after 5.0
(scarthgap). If you are migrating from the 5.0 version, be sure to read the
previous migration guides:

-  /migration-guides/migration-5.1
-  /migration-guides/migration-5.2
-  /migration-guides/migration-5.3

See also the list of new features and enhancements of the previous releases:

-  /migration-guides/release-notes-5.1
-  /migration-guides/release-notes-5.2
-  /migration-guides/release-notes-5.3

### New Features / Enhancements in |yocto-ver|

-  Linux kernel XXX, gcc XXX, glibc XXX, LLVM XXX, and over XXX other
   recipe upgrades

-  Minimum Python version required on the host: XXX.

-  New variables:

   -  The **OPENSSH_HOST_KEY_DIR** variable can be used to specify the
      directory where OpenSSH host keys are stored. The default value is
      `/etc/ssh` (`addd80ddfd892eb4513f323d369d210935416e05`)

   -  The **OPENSSH_HOST_KEY_DIR_READONLY_CONFIG** variable can be used to
      specify the directory where OpenSSH host keys are stored when the device
      uses a read-only filesystem. The default value is `/var/run/ssh`
      (`addd80ddfd892eb4513f323d369d210935416e05`)

   -  The **SPDX_INCLUDE_KERNEL_CONFIG** can be set to "1" to export the Linux
      kernel configuration (`CONFIG_*` parameters) into the SPDX document when
      using the ref-classes-create-spdx class
      (`228a968e7c47d811c06143279bdb0f9c5f374bef`)

   -  The **SPDX_INCLUDE_PACKAGECONFIG** variable can be set to "1" to
      export a recipe's **PACKAGECONFIG** features (enabled/disabled) into
      the SPDX document when using the ref-classes-create-spdx class
      (`228a968e7c47d811c06143279bdb0f9c5f374bef`)

   -  The **SPDX_PACKAGE_URL** allows specifying a space-separated list of
      Package URLs (purls) for the software Package when using the
      ref-classes-create-spdx class
      (`874b2d301d3cac617b1028bc6ce91b1f916a6508`)

   -  The **SPDX_CONCLUDED_LICENSE** allows specifying the
      `hasConcludedLicense` object of individual SBOM packages when using the
      ref-classes-create-spdx class
      (`bb21c6a429a2ecec82a8afe7d79502291ccaef01`)

   -  The **FIT_MKIMAGE_EXTRA_OPTS** variable allows passing extra options
      to the `mkimage` command when creating a FIT image with the
      ref-classes-kernel-fit-image class
      (`d925d67061ef5d7a8abe15c715614650094d50c6`)

   -  The **FIT_CONF_MAPPINGS** variable allows mapping extra configurations
      to existing ones or rename an existing configuration in FIT images created
      with the ref-classes-kernel-fit-image class (`e30f809a50c2151e525424879383c02325a7ec9a`)

   -  The **UBOOT_CONFIG_FRAGMENTS** and **UBOOT_FRAGMENTS** allow
      supplying additional configuration fragments to the existing U-Boot
      configuration. See the definition of the variables for more information,
      and the documentation of the ref-classes-uboot-config class
      (`9e96d3dedee47657657686310508e0aaee7f4e02`)

   -  The **IMAGE_EXTRA_PARTITION_FILES** allows specifying extra files from
      the deploy directory (**DEPLOY_DIR_IMAGE**) to install in a WIC
      partition created with the `extra_partition` plugin
      (`e1526079d205dac6e3cff6d8e5cb37f68b631009`)

-  Kernel-related changes:

-  New core recipes:

   -  `libconfig`: Import recipe from `/meta-openembedded/`, needed by
      one of the Mesa recipes (`1a0196a794f8858c4715871558e97c3d69deb19e`)

   -  `python3-sphinxcontrib-svg2pdfconverter`: Used for the generation of the
      Yocto Project documentation (`f3f001967744b593fb39c32058d29595bbf0ffb6`)

   -  `python3-pyzstd`: Import from :oe_git:`meta-python
      </meta-openembedded/tree/meta-python>`, needed by the `ukify` tool of
      systemd v258 (`88a27133c184125e1503d1397cbd276a9a76f6ab`)

   -  `python3-uv-build`: This recipe adds the `uv` Python build backend,
      required by `python3-cryptography` (`0880cd2b79ee47274d62035e354aa4b93966b4d0`)

   -  `blueprint-compiler`: Add the recipe as it became a dependency of the
      `epiphany` recipe after its upgrade to 49.2 (`4212392ca7ebf890e1e192ddd0e7dbe1f8dabcf2`)

-  New core classes:

-  Global configuration changes:

   -  `base-passwd`: Add a `clock` group as `systemd` version v258 introduces
      this group to `enable applications like linuxptp to open clocks without
      root privileges <https://github.com/systemd/systemd/commit/af96ccfc24bc4803078a46b4ef2cdeb5decdfbcd>`__
      (`aad849301be157b5605fc0133e7312ca30250d82`)

   -  `lto.inc`: Add a `Clang` specific **LTO** configuration
      (`253da2e6fc0aa01cbd1b249cfcca35d9fe7740ba`)

   -  `bitbake.conf`:

      -  remove **DEBUG_PREFIX_MAP** from **TARGET_LDFLAGS**
         (`1797741aad02b8bf429fac4b81e30cdda64b5448`)

      -  The default definition of **TARGET_LDFLAGS** used to
         contain the value of **DEBUG_PREFIX_MAP**, to fix binary
         reproducibility issues. This was no longer needed after the originating
         `GCC bug <https://gcc.gnu.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=101473>`__ was
         fixed (`1797741aad02b8bf429fac4b81e30cdda64b5448`)

      -  Switch **BB_SIGNATURE_HANDLER** to `OEEquivHash` and
         **BB_HASHSERVE** to `auto` by default
         (`5596ea156d3f2abea57e590798bbbf1bf4a860de`,
         `4a388406acf0210e8a47c4733979256b10e078ff`)

   -  The ref-classes-uninative class is now enabled by default. This
      allows reuse of native sstate built on one distro on another
      (`722897f96d30e978b20e140419fb044d850f5c74`)

   -  The :oe_git:`no-static-libs.inc
      <openembedded-core/tree/meta/conf/distro/include/no-static-libs.inc>`
      file, disabling most static libraries in various recipes, is now included
      by default in the default distro setup (appearing as the `nodistro`
      **DISTRO**) (`03fc931bfe9ea3fa9f33553e6020cbc067b24291`)

   -  The :oe_git:`security_flags.inc
      <openembedded-core/tree/meta/conf/distro/include/security_flags.inc>`
      file, adding various security related flags to the default compiler and
      linker, is now included by default in the default distro setup (appearing
      as `nodistro` **DISTRO**)
      (`4c2d64c10a5b0437ab1ea04df22386f0f95124d1`)

   -  The :oe_git:`yocto-space-optimize.inc
      <openembedded-core/tree/meta/conf/distro/include/yocto-space-optimize.inc>`
      file, adding various space optimization tweaks, is now included by default
      in the default distro setup (appearing as `nodistro` **DISTRO**)
      (`175fcf9fad699dd122680d3f6961af9bf8487046`)

-  Architecture-specific changes:

-  QEMU / `runqemu` changes:

   -  `qemuboot``: Make the tap interface nameserver configurable through
      **QB_TAP_NAMESERVER**
      (`0e8c2582d46dc703511521386d1cc1694ae21e22`)

   -  `qemu`: Disable the `libkeyutils` feature
      (`30cc9f5192436f5498ff9c55c0545eebb50fa423`)

   -  `runqemu-extract-sdk`: Support the `tar.zst` format
      (`650bb45251b518847fd998891d5b6b5989fb7cd8`)

   -  `qemurunner`: Improve `qmp` module detection
      (`a7386d071d42c7cd8413a957d1f72d73838ad6e7`)

   -  `runqemu`: Support `.tar.zst`, `.tar,xz`, `.tar` rootfs archive
      types (`3a6172fbb6d3866b84627bcbf13e0a96837a85b1`)

-  Documentation changes:

-  Go changes:

   -  ref-classes-go: Disable workspaces when building
      (`GOWORK="off"`) (`c52c5e88626968b08510818f09829f2e1c9f94ae`)

   -  `meta-go-toolchain`: Disable support for the `riscv32` and `ppc32`
      architectures, as this was not supported
      (`f55407185c63c895fa3c4fdf74e6e63ea9517a20`)

-  Rust changes:

   -  Enable dynamic linking with llvm. This allows dynamically linking to
      `libLLVM.so` instead of linking statically
      (`74ba238ff1ba1e9b612aece1989b828f3a8f8770`)

-  Wic Image Creator changes:

   -  `wic/engine`: Fix copying directories into wic image with `ext*`
      partitions (`1ed38aff5f810d064c87aff9cbd310906833b6ba`)

-  SDK-related changes:

-  Testing-related changes:

   - ref-classes-ptest support was added for the following recipes:

      -  `libarchive` (`6e0bf90e31c969fb18efb18aaceaee886194a7b7`)
      -  `libassuan` (`1010abf3e32e6616ef0075d4d826c1734937152b`)
      -  `libcheck` (`1bb06e23c1c87829e5c4211e34229305c7d5f851`)
      -  `libconfig` (`f3e9d1326bf37361ff94dc4eef52de13b64651b2`)
      -  `libksba` (`f50a2005dda8cecf3a9db44edb131e7e332fa42d`)
      -  `libmd` (`4c0a41389bdab30e3b349fef8df6ca0ef4893b89`)
      -  `libsolv` (`f5432d1c45f9eb47182049c6930cfc6d5b26bc8d`)
      -  `libyaml` (`ed2a3459829bb3b6c10143cceaef0147a0cb2b98`)
      -  `utfcpp` (`49314caa7eb8efd86577121337a0b0d7472eab1b`)

   -  `selftests`: Use SHA256 keys for RPM tests
      (`692919be1947b95a73b9655977396c4f65f811c4`)

   -  `oeqa`: Open JSON files to parse in a context manager
      (`e96baf588dfa90d366e94f2a72ec8941e397c596`)

   -  `resulttool`: Add ref-classes-ptest support to the JUnit output
      format (`2abe2d701c98cbf3a81f211252157de523742b0b`)

   -  ref-tasks-testimage: Print last lines of kernel log on test fail
      (`fea3c445a9ccb803468b83ea4e8fa92fe442b8e0`)

   -  `reproducible`: Use the `jQuery` CDN instead of `jquery-native`
      (`d3ee5497b1ce6eb487419f6d821c3ad38491e5ec`)

   -  `selftest`: Test installation of recipes with complex packaging
      (`6f3aab6bfa754ecaeee0acc013cb6be1f07c1ec0`)

-  Utility script changes:

   -  `bitbake-config-build`: It is now possible to disable all fragments
      starting with a prefix by issuing ``bitbake-config-build disable-fragment
      <prefix>/`` (`573695d2ff3e0d47c6ef91418e5002df017bb7bc`)

   -  `recipetool`: Support PEP639-variant of license key in Python
      `pyproject.toml` files (`9d1a7bb5d8aa94b74cd66edcb88e323c926d299b`)

   -  ref-classes-buildhistory:

      -  Also show renamed directories (`9bf22112ea4687e4bf855f12b592b37479aa40df`)
      -  Fix handling of **RDEPENDS** style strings (`b013d62d1092a5f2ed14c11d6e7bb37d74e5e6cc`)

   -  `create-pull-request`: Keep commit hash to be pulled in cover email
      (`c78f5ae4a5ba3675b78cc226feb7b9fbbfd8da19`)

   -  `yocto-check-layer`: Add messages in `test_readme` assertions
      (`9fe883ce4c6284f1b75031adafeeafb47e56958c`)

-  BitBake changes:

   -  `bitbake-layers`:

      -  Add a `--show-variants` option to the `show-recipes` subcommand to
         display **BBCLASSEXTEND** variants
         (`353d5e948c99a5d4f76f414054aca039a78e7ab9`)

      -  Fix the branch detection method of `layerindex-fetch`
         (`af9dd012e7f4d16dccd1d6118be5da94ede68f85`)

   -  `bitbake-setup`:

      -  Implement symlinking local sources into builds with the
         `--use-local-source` option of the `init` subcommand
         (`ed5a3a0fc82041c95a8104ba8a123f7eb1c19e57`)

      -  Convert `print()` calls to use a **BitBake** logger
         (`6e511d035a3d1e4129dab7b0dfbf216bd8e99b47`)

      -  Correct several scenarios in layer updates
         (`aa15cc7bd10264ca28aabd3b5a652d818efc389e`)

      -  Source in the `git-remote` section can now be specified more simply
         with the `uri` property, instead of the `remotes` property
         (`7941a5dc4dba81ab2141531b8af94371a923b32b`). For example:

         .. code-block:: json

            "bitbake": {
                "git-remote": {
                    "uri": "https://git.openembedded.org/bitbake",
                    "branch": "master",
                    "rev": "master"
                }
            }

      -  Use the internal registry if run from a Git checkout, from a remote
         **BitBake** repository otherwise
         (`675e9076a25248d49f01d7877a78f5a08a9daabc`)

      -  Fragments passed in the `oe-fragments-one-of` property can now
         contain descriptions (`29f2cee655be31c401e30ad818a1c4b10458b530`)

      -  Improve the readability of choices during the
         bitbake:ref-bbsetup-command-init command
         (`d9700632bd6b627d1124fdc83ddf7bfb4199228d`)

      -  Enable coloring of the diff outputs when using the
         bitbake:ref-bbsetup-command-status or
         bitbake:ref-bbsetup-command-update commands

   -  `cooker`: Use **bitbake:BB_HASHSERVE_DB_DIR** as hash server
      database location. If unset, the existing behavior is preserved
      (`b339d05ad2b69a6518522ee4c46dd5f5a6e33f65`)

   -  `bitbake-getvar`: Show close matches when no providers are found
      (`1f8fa7c25e71cd0f230a2f6bfd9d5153c694da81`)

-  Packaging changes:

-  Clang/LLVM related changes:

   -  `compiler-rt`: 

      -  Remove the need to depend on `libgcc`
         (`f504b6bb8366019d46e48efc5f3fa79859476431`)
      -  Always build C runtime (`crt`) files
         (`56fe42abe21eb5a5f809abb9893d8f33dac8bc12`)

   -  `libcxx`: Remove GNU runtime from dependencies
      (`8034509d30657ce40eb0773c4cf39c0e77af84c8`)

   -  `libcxx/compiler-rt`: Add support for `llvm-libgcc`, a drop-in
      replacement for `libgcc` and `crt` files
      (`ed02230e3bba030b227e3af0c5438d00800d3457`)

-  SPDX-related changes:

   -  `spdx30_tasks`: Fix **SPDX_CUSTOM_ANNOTATION_VARS** implementation
      (`52ab3b640c6bb7ece34cb4ea6026fd6375f17af4`)

   -  ref-classes-kernel: Add a task to export the kernel configuration
      to SPDX (`228a968e7c47d811c06143279bdb0f9c5f374bef`)

   -  Add support for exporting the **PACKAGECONFIG** to SPDX
      (`7ec61ac40345a5c0ef1ce20513a4596989c91ef4`)

   -  Add suport for package URLs (PURLs) through **SPDX_PACKAGE_URL**
      (`874b2d301d3cac617b1028bc6ce91b1f916a6508`)

   -  `create-spdx-2.2`: Add CVEs in **CVE_CHECK_IGNORE**
      to the list of fixed CVEs in the output SBOM
      (`f8525224cb825b1aad2be240731eabafdde7612d`)

-  `devtool` changes:

   -  `ide-sdk`: Find `bitbake-setup`'s `init-build-env`
      first, and `oe-init-build-env` if not found
      (`6ab7e9e8e52fa123551438820c59b8c5e0c9c8a5`)

   -  `ide-sdk`: Add `gdbserver` attach mode support
      (`119171087681bd47842865d6451868c1127f1149`)

   -  `ide-sdk`: Support GDB pretty-printing for C++ STL types
      (`a69e2baba81b0cd88d58b164433c72e1156424b1`)

-  Patchtest-related changes:

   -  Code refactoring and improvements
      (`86d0b2254ae9dc5bf9d19469c7ef71f8129fbf93`,
      `317ef42b9b2324847574f62b1ec3627ffcf76e38`,
      `6cdb5cbbee6281af1b71407da0d0af74dc7b9631`,
      `ae787b32d501d8dce85c26c01229297f9184b7f8`,
      `a850252348096e5d6b0bb267e5108bf73de88e85`)

   -  Reject `Upstream-Status` after scissors
      (`2156ef9e6defa3ec9087789fcea25fb4fee7b83c`)

-  ref-classes-insane / ref-classes-sanity classes related changes:

-  Security changes:

   -  A new document was added to the Yocto Project documentation:
      /security-reference/index. It is intended to document how to report
      vulnerabilities to the Yocto Project security team.

-  ref-classes-cve-check-related changes:

   -  `cve-update-nvd2-native`: Use maximum CVSS score when extracting it from
      multiple sources (`4f6192f3165de0bc2499e045607c7e7ffd878a4b`)

-  New **PACKAGECONFIG** options for individual recipes:

   -  `curl`: `schannel`
   -  `gstreamer1.0-plugins-good`: `qt6`
   -  `libinput`: `lua`, `libwacom`, `mtdev`
   -  `mesa`: `expat`, `zlib`
   -  `openssl`: `legacy`
   -  `opkg`: `acl`, `xattr`
   -  `python3-cryptography`: `legacy-openssl`

-  systemd related changes:

   -  Package `ukify` separately, with the `systemd-ukify` package name
      (`e9242749621040accba8252d50c036b3e4b10e09`)

-  U-Boot related changes:

   -  ref-classes-uboot-config: Add support for generating the U-Boot
      initial environment in binary format using
      **UBOOT_INITIAL_ENV_BINARY**
      (`cf11b14a4cfc0617f45f7cdb87d1dec4aa58e765`)

   -  A new way of specifying multiple U-Boot configurations has been added
      (`cd9e7304481b24b27df61c03ad73496d18e4d47c`). See
      ref-migration-6-0-u-boot-config-flow-changes

-  Miscellaneous changes:

   -  `curl`: Ensure `CURL_CA_BUNDLE` from host environment is respected
      (`545e43a7a45be02fda8fc3af69faa20e889f58c4`)

   -  `weston`: Add PipeWire as runtime dependency when `pipewire` is part
      of **PACKAGECONFIG**
      (`9f5286725ad4c3ab241ce7992f992d4e81acc81e`)

   -  ref-classes-uki: Use basename of device trees available via
      **KERNEL_DEVICETREE**
      (`27a7fbb767c0a25b34a03cae90320908f8ade8de`)

   -  `rpcbind`: Set the owner of `/run/rpcbind` to `rpc`
      (`80e428924715fa954fc68c381cb0aea19e73a5b8`)

   -  ref-classes-archiver: Remove **WORKDIR** from the patch
      directory (`c99d22827c9515e9fdb31d4989925aa9e9604134`)

   -  `gtk4`: Convert to use the ref-classes-gnomebase class
      (`fcd5e7c4468fe28b8d1e22ba134346bf92ddbe1c`)

   -  `udev-extraconf`: Split `automount` and `autonet` into seperate
      packages (`udev-extraconf-automount` and `udev-extraconf-autonet`)
      (`08662d71cd357c29c47dc42ead1d9106c584a1b8`)

   -  `e2fsprogs`: Fix a bug for files larger than 2GB
      (`683a1e773899f3042458604b3f136861318c1028`)

   -  `mesa`: Add support for the `virtio`, `gfxstream`, `hasvk` Vulkan drivers
      (`8e7ffdceded33091e72c9a3ceb239d847cf917a9`,
      `3b56f14b2019c42e0efce2a8d10cb7aeaf782da8`)

   -  `mesa`: Drop VDPAU  remnants in the recipe after
      upstream support was removed (`3b05f58586bc3cc156c194342fe1775e567870d1`)

   -  ref-classes-cross: Propagate dependencies to `outhash`,
      improving :ref:`hash equivalence <overview-manual/concepts:Hash
      Equivalence>` (`267b651e875d9381a23ffd5757d426714c029409`)

   -  `run-postinsts`: Propagate exit status to the `run-postinsts.service`
      systemd service (`7f74d88bb628f186309c9228cf01293b046e43ca`)

   -  `freetype`: Use ref-classes-meson instead of
      ref-classes-autotools (`7395e4f99e90063dbb9c07b62ddffd824fba84fc`)

   -  `wpa-supplicant`:

      -  Build with OWE  support
         by default (`d16c66b4efada276536ccd3c8456f02ab9753e2e`)

      -  Build with 802.11be  support by default
         (`d16c66b4efada276536ccd3c8456f02ab9753e2e`)

   -  `overlayfs`: Remove helper unit
      (`623c20ff1e989730138c3fbe6e8247eaada20707`)

   -  ref-classes-patch: Show full path when a patch fails to apply
      (`602e28b4813479f87d3f949e5cf23b34fc34a478`)

   -  `kea`: Replace `keactrl` with `kea` daemons (`kea-dhcp*`) in
      initscripts (`7f9d9297a84f8f5dc08bc310f825ac8a4acf5452`), and
      remove `keactrl` from the recipe
      (`08c3877f4df8392ae347b03ac5334b170b1a4fec`)

   -  `initramfs-framework`: Add handover of PID 1's arguments to modules
      (`a0ab3d1c4f9ed34d1d17e6534f42d17b3387ebb3`)

   -  `perl`: Provide `pod2man` (in the recipe's **PROVIDES**
      definition). This is used by many other recipes to produce :wikipedia:`man
      pages <Man_page>`. This allows existing recipes to explicitly depend on
      `pod2man-native` to produce man pages
      (`1d1e55d200fb0363d1cb96cc1323a407f3b93349`)

   -  `build-sysroots`: Add sysroot tasks to default build and remove warning
      (`e73f1509552285b628477267824e48eb79790fb7`)

   -  Licenses and manifests are now deployed in the SDK when setting
      **COPY_LIC_DIRS** and/or **COPY_LIC_MANIFEST**, for both host and
      target sysroots (`f757ae4dadabd09cfa056bd34172c09ca3693441`)

   -  `openssl`: Disable TLS 1.0/1.1 by default
      (`d5501e77208825b6ebffe51e8d680cdd84cfd0ab`)

   -  `python3-cryptography`: Disable `legacy-openssl` feature by default
      (`1acd1998bbaf6b346c756aea7c11916e5e22fbbb`)

   -  `openssl`: Add support for config snippet includes. This can be done by
      installing extra configuration files in `${sysconfdir}/ssl/openssl.cnf.d/`
      (`34bafcf3d8cdaa87506df30ef554d18981454c5e`)

   -  `busybox`: Enable SELinux support if **DISTRO_FEATURES** contains
      `selinux` (`c544f12073ea712c3d3ce08105d52640a7a322b9`)

   -  `coreutils`: `kill` and `uptime` are no longer provided by the
      recipe (`cedeb958dfa892e409bdce8525030c20b3400332`)

### Known Issues in |yocto-ver|

### Recipe License changes in |yocto-ver|

The following changes have been made to the **LICENSE** values set by recipes:

.. list-table::
   :widths: 20 40 40
   :header-rows: 1

   * - Recipe
     - Previous value
     - New value
   * - `recipe name`
     - Previous value
     - New value

### Security Fixes in |yocto-ver|

The following CVEs have been fixed:

.. list-table::
   :widths: 30 70
   :header-rows: 1

   * - Recipe
     - CVE IDs
   * - `recipe name`
     - `xxx-xxxx`, ...

### Recipe Upgrades in |yocto-ver|

The following recipes have been upgraded:

.. list-table::
   :widths: 20 40 40
   :header-rows: 1

   * - Recipe
     - Previous version
     - New version
   * - `recipe name`
     - Previous version
     - New version
